title: Bridge Essay: Neuroinflammation, metabolic dysfunction, and chronic stress converge on the insular cortex in chronic pain.
concept: Spiritus as interoceptive inference — heart-brain coupling, energy metabolism as felt presence.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Body-energy** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Spiritus as interoceptive inference — heart-brain coupling, energy metabolism as felt presence.

**Neuroinflammation, metabolic dysfunction, and chronic stress converge on the insular cortex in chronic pain.** - Neuroscience and biobehavioral reviews (2026). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2026.106791. PMID: 42235664. Chronic pain is a prevalent cause of disability worldwide and is increasingly recognized as a complex condition involving alterations in neural processing, emotional regulation, and bodily awareness. Among the brain regions implicated in persistent pain states, the insular cortex has consistently been identified as the most frequently affected cortical region, playing a central role in the integration and interpretation of interoceptive signals.
